name: Centro Btt Izki Ruta 9 – circular desde Peñacerrada
region_intro: Mountain biking around Peñacerrada-Urizaharra offers diverse terrain at the foot of the Sierra de Cantabria, with Mount Toloño as a prominent feature. The region features rugged landscapes, including challenging climbs and scenic trails, with the Inglares River flowing through the area. Riders can explore routes through vineyards and near the Izki Natural Park, providing options for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Peñacerrada-Urizaharra The most popular mountain bike trail is Centro Btt Izki Ruta 9 – circular desde Peñacerrada, a 21.2 miles (34.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 29 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Izki Natural Park area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Labastida – Zapiturri Fountain loop from Labastida / Bastida, a difficult 24.4 miles (39.3 km) path. This demanding route features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Puerto de Herrera – Ollerías Waterfall loop from Salinillas de Buradón / Gatzaga Buradon, a 32.3 miles (51.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 42 minutes. Mountain biking around Peñacerrada-Urizaharra is defined by the Sierra de Cantabria, Mount Toloño, and routes through vineyards, with options for both challenging climbs and scenic paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate gravel rides to difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in Peñacerrada-Urizaharra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Peñacerrada-Urizaharra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Peñacerrada-Urizaharra?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike trails around Peñacerrada-Urizaharra, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Peñacerrada-Urizaharra?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from challenging climbs in the Sierra de Cantabria and around Mount Toloño to scenic trails along the Inglares River. You'll find routes through rugged landscapes, vineyards, and even hard gravel rides, catering to various fitness and skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Peñacerrada-Urizaharra?
A: Yes, there are 10 easy mountain bike trails in the area, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. Additionally, 50 moderate trails offer a good step up for improving skills.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike trails in Peñacerrada-Urizaharra?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056807835/centro-btt-izki-ruta-9-circular-desde-penacerrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centro Btt Izki Ruta 9 – circular desde Peñacerrada&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult circular route exploring the Izki Natural Park area. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40330720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Labastida – Zapiturri Fountain loop from Labastida / Bastida&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a challenging circular path.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: You can encounter several notable natural features. Many routes offer views of the Sierra de Cantabria and Mount Toloño, such as the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3223322" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toloño&lt;/a&gt;. The Inglares River flows through the area, and some trails lead to points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Herrera – Ollerías Waterfall loop from Salinillas de Buradón / Gatzaga Buradon&lt;/a&gt;, which features a waterfall.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. Peñacerrada-Urizaharra itself is a medieval village with a walled enclosure and historical buildings like the Church of the Assumption. Many routes pass through or near the Rioja Alavesa wine region, offering opportunities to visit wineries in towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5184637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haro&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5557742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bodegas Ysios&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Peñacerrada-Urizaharra?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region is generally excellent for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and stable weather conditions, making them ideal for mountain biking. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring colder temperatures, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: While the guide highlights more challenging routes, the presence of 10 easy trails suggests there are options suitable for families or those with less experience. These easier paths often feature less elevation gain and smoother surfaces, making them more accessible.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Peñacerrada-Urizaharra?
A: The mountain bike routes in Peñacerrada-Urizaharra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from challenging climbs to scenic paths through vineyards, and the overall natural beauty of the Sierra de Cantabria region.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass through vineyards?
A: Yes, the region's proximity to Rioja Alavesa means many routes traverse vineyards. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38195713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haro – Puente de Cihuri loop from Labastida / Bastida&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you through this renowned wine region.

Q: Are there any advanced or difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders will find plenty of challenges. There are 26 difficult mountain bike trails in the area. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056807835/centro-btt-izki-ruta-9-circular-desde-penacerrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centro Btt Izki Ruta 9 – circular desde Peñacerrada&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Herrera – Ollerías Waterfall loop from Salinillas de Buradón / Gatzaga Buradon&lt;/a&gt;, both offering significant elevation gain and demanding terrain.
tips: Forest and more wonderful forest. The trail here is shady and beautiful. It's not hard to follow and really nice.

At this point you will see a pile of stones. There are several different trails that come together here, including the GR38. This is also where you enter Castilla y León for a very short section of trail. Also, there is a field near here that takes you out of the woods, but only by a few meters.
A bit off the main path you will find La Rasa, which is almost 1000 meters above sea level and has very nice views over the trees. It also has a very curious and interesting mailbox.
